sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: 블랙록, 2025년 AUM 14조 달러 돌파·사상 최대 순유입 6,980억 달러, 연간 조정 주당순이익 48.09달러

keyPoints.text: 2025년 말 운용자산(AUM)이 14조 달러를 넘어섰고, 연간 순유입은 사상 최대인 6,980억 달러를 기록했습니다.
연간 조정 주당순이익은 48.09달러, GAAP 기준 35.31달러였으며 4분기 조정 주당순이익은 13.16달러를 기록했습니다.
고객이 2025년 맡긴 신규 자산 6,980억 달러가 9%의 유기적 기본수수료 성장을 견인했고 4분기 유기적 성장률은 12%에 달했습니다.

businessChanges.text: 성장과 마진 개선 전망을 바탕으로 주당 배당을 10% 인상하고 자사주 매입 계획을 확대했으며, 2026년은 GIP·HPS·Preqin을 통합한 첫 완전한 해가 됩니다.

financialHighlights.text: 연간 조정 영업이익은 18% 증가했고 조정 주당순이익은 10% 늘었으나, GAAP 영업이익은 비현금 비용 영향으로 7% 감소했습니다.

watchItems.text: GIP·HPS·Preqin 통합이 본격화되는 2026년에 프라이빗 마켓 부문의 성장 기여가 실현되는지가 핵심 관전 포인트입니다.
비현금 인수 비용으로 인한 GAAP 이익 감소가 향후 정상화되는지 주목할 필요가 있습니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: EX-99.1 2 blk-ex99_1.htm EX-99.1 EX-99.1 Exhibit 99.1 INVESTOR RELATIONS: Caroline Rodda 212.810.3442 MEDIA RELATIONS: Patrick Scanlan 212.810.3622 BlackRock Reports Full Year 2025 Diluted EPS of $35.31, or $48.09 as adjusted Fourth Quarter 2025 Diluted EPS of $7.16, or $13.16 as adjusted New York, January 15, 2026 – BlackRock, Inc. (NYSE: BLK) today reported financial results for the three months and year ended December 31, 2025. $14 trillion in AUM following record $698 billion of full year net inflows, including $342 billion in the fourth quarter 12% annualized organic base fee growth in the fourth quarter reflects broad-based strength across iShares ® ETFs, systematic active equities, private markets, outsourcing and cash 19% increase in full year revenue reflects the pos  Normalized numeric aliases: $14 trillion = 14조 달러; $698 billion = 6,980억 달러; $342 billion = 3,420억 달러; $35.31 = 35.31달러; $48.09 = 48.09달러; $7.16 = 7.16달러; $13.16 = 13.16달러; $14 = 14달러; $342 = 342달러; $698 = 698달러.

ransactions, and higher technology services and subscription revenue 7% decrease in full year GAAP operating income and 16% decrease in GAAP diluted EPS related to noncash acquisition-related expenses and a noncash charitable contribution, which have been excluded from as adjusted results 18% increase in full year as adjusted operating income driven by higher revenue 10% increase in full year as adjusted diluted EPS also reflects lower nonoperating income and a higher diluted share count $5 billion returned to shareholders in 2025, including $1.6 billion worth of share repurchases 10% increase in quarterly cash dividend to $5.73 per share approved by Board of Directors, payable March 24, 2026 to shareholders of record at the close of business on March 6, 2026 7 million additional shares authorized for repurchase under existing repurchase program by Board  Normalized numeric aliases: $5 billion = 50억 달러; $1.6 billion = 16억 달러; 7 million = 700만 달러; $5 = 5달러; $1.6 = 1.6달러; $5.73 = 5.73달러.

airman and CEO: “BlackRock enters 2026 with accelerating momentum across our entire platform, coming off the strongest year and quarter of net inflows in our history. Clients entrusted us with $698 billion of new assets in 2025, powering 9% organic base fee growth. And we ended the year with back-to-back quarters of double-digit organic base fee growth, including 12% in the fourth quarter. “2026 will be our first full year as a unified platform with GIP, HPS and Preqin. Around the world, clients are looking to do more across BlackRock. Our pipeline of business has broadened across products and regions, spanning public and private markets mandates, technology and data, and client channels. We’re seeing excellent fundraising activity as we work toward our goal of $400 billion in private markets fundraising by 2030. “BlackRock is at the forefront of some of the largest new growth c  Normalized numeric aliases: $698 billion = 6,980억 달러; $400 billion = 4,000억 달러; $698 = 698달러; $400 = 400달러.

cross the industry – from private markets to wealth and 401(k), to active ETFs, to private markets data, to digital assets and tokenization. Our belief in our increasing growth and margin trajectory led us to raise our dividend per share by 10% and our level of planned share repurchases. “BlackRock is differentiated as a scale operator in public and private markets investing and technology, which is enhancing our positioning with clients worldwide. We’re a leader in public and private markets, and in technology and data. We’re a foundational player in both traditional and decentralized financial markets. Importantly, we bring it all together to deliver BlackRock to our clients in a consistent, unified way. We’ve already begun 2026 with strong momentum, and we’re positioned ahead of big future opportunities to deliver better outcomes for clients and growth for our shareholders.” FINANCIAL
